scene_summary: A tall nocturne plate built from nested architectural apertures: a glowing ochre vertical window at left, a dense black slab down the center, a pale observatory dome at upper right, and small scuffs, lights, panes, rails, and folded wall marks embedded as source-bearing edges.
usable_surfaces: cyan-dark side pane in left recess
upper observatory aperture and balcony rail
low diagonal scrape on floor edge
speckled node marks on dark slab

detected_objects.label: hero ochre viewing aperture
detected_objects.visual_evidence: large warm ochre illuminated vertical window plane set inside a deep left wall reveal, with clear inner edges and glowing textured surface

detected_objects.label: hero dark algorithm slab
detected_objects.visual_evidence: dominant black-brown vertical slab descending through the composition, with irregular painted edges and dense dark texture

detected_objects.label: left portrait-glow mullion
detected_objects.visual_evidence: pale vertical glass/mullion edge bordering the tall left opening, separating the dark exterior view from the ochre interior glow

detected_objects.label: pool-cyan side pane
detected_objects.visual_evidence: cool blue-dark recessed pane at far left showing dusk sky, trees, and distant lights, framed as a distinct vertical aperture

detected_objects.label: converging ochre rail break
detected_objects.visual_evidence: thin balcony/guard rail fragments and floor seam recede across the middle room, forming a narrow physical corridor edge

detected_objects.label: low diagonal blob trace
detected_objects.visual_evidence: Planned source surface fallback used because plate inspection returned fewer mapped objects than source bindings.

complexity_assessment.rationale: The plate remains expressionist and image-led, but the nested rooms, telescope dome, ladder, railings, lights, and many small scuffs create a busy architectural field. The strongest surfaces still read clearly: ochre aperture, black slab, left pane, and observatory opening.

mapping_note: Package assembly filled unmapped source pockets from planned artifact surfaces because plate inspection returned too few detected objects.
